steps.title: Find the key signal in the “but”
steps.content:
The passage states Renata was eager to promote Quechua in her music, but she was ______ speaking it.

The word “but” signals a contrast—something that works against her eagerness.

The passage then reveals her response: asking her grandmother to teach her pronunciation and taking language classes.

These actions of seeking help for basic language skills directly show what blocked her: she lacked experience in actually speaking the language.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
“Inexperienced with” directly names the gap the passage describes.

A person seeking pronunciation help and enrolling in classes to learn a language is, by definition, inexperienced with it.

This choice creates a logical contrast: eager in purpose (promoting the language) but inexperienced in execution (speaking it).

The other three choices contradict this narrative—”prepared for” and “skilled in” describe someone who would not need that help; “excited about” is redundant with “eager” and fails to explain why she needed to take classes.

That matches Choice B — inexperienced with.
